questions.text: Do you have a project idea you would like to build on MST? If yes, describe your idea briefly (150 - 300 words) and identify which MST grant track (initGrant, buildGrant, or scaleGrant) it currently fits. Justify your choice by referencing the specific stage requirements (e.g., concept only → initGrant, testnet MVP → buildGrant, mainnet live → scaleGrant). If you don't have an idea yet, propose a hypothetical one using the same format.
questions.explanation: 📊 Marking Rubric - How Your Answer Will Be Evaluated
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Rubric - How Your Answer Will Be Evaluated
Criteria What Earns Full Marks Marks
1. Idea clarity & feasibility The idea is specific, solves a clear problem, and uses blockchain (preferably MST features) appropriately. It is not a vague "decentralised Uber" clone. 1 mark
2. Correct grant track selection The chosen track (initGrant / buildGrant / scaleGrant) matches the project's current maturity level as described in the answer (e.g., concept only → initGrant). 1 mark
3. Justification based on stage requirements The answer explicitly references the specific entry requirements of the chosen track (code deployed? users? mainnet?). It explains why a lower or higher track is not appropriate. 2 marks
4. Structure & writing quality The answer is well-organized, within the word limit, free of major grammatical errors, and includes a brief conclusion. 1 mark
Examiner note: If the student selects an obviously incorrect track (e.g., choosing scaleGrant for a sketch on a napkin), 0 marks for criteria 2 and 3. Partial marks are possible for a correct track with weak justification.
